questions.question: Q1. After performing the simulation, which change most effectively reduced queue length at the intersection?

explanations.a: Correct. A longer green signal allows more vehicles to pass, reducing queue length.
explanations.b: Longer red times generally increase queue length.

questions.question: Q2. What happens when the vehicle arrival rate becomes greater than the service rate?

explanations.a: Correct. More vehicles arrive than can be cleared, causing congestion.

questions.question: Q3. How does increasing the number of lanes affect intersection performance?

explanations.a: Correct. Additional lanes increase the number of vehicles that can pass.

questions.question: Q4. Which output parameter is used to measure the effectiveness of traffic signal operation?

explanations.a: Correct. Signal Efficiency measures how effectively vehicles are managed at the intersection.
explanations.b: Road width is an infrastructure parameter.

questions.question: Q5. What is the most likely effect of increasing pedestrian flow at an intersection?

explanations.a: Correct. More pedestrian crossings can reduce time available for vehicle movement.
explanations.b: Pedestrian flow does not increase capacity.

questions.question: Q6. A high traffic density value indicates that:

answers.a: The road is crowded with vehicles

explanations.a: Correct. High density means many vehicles occupy the available road space.
explanations.b: An empty road has low density.
explanations.c: Signal operation does not determine density.

questions.question: Q7. If the green signal time is too short during heavy traffic, what will happen?

answers.a: Queue length and waiting time will increase
answers.b: All vehicles will clear the intersection

explanations.a: Correct. Insufficient green time causes vehicle accumulation.
explanations.b: Heavy traffic cannot be cleared with short green time.
explanations.d: Signal timing does not increase road capacity.

questions.question: Q8. Which output parameter represents the number of vehicles successfully crossing the intersection?

explanations.a: Correct. Vehicle Throughput measures vehicles passing through the intersection.

questions.question: Q9. Which engineering concept is primarily demonstrated when vehicles form a line while waiting at a red signal?

explanations.a: Correct. Queueing Theory studies waiting lines and service systems.
explanations.b: Thermodynamics deals with heat and energy.

questions.question: Q10. Based on the experiment, what is the main benefit of traffic signal optimization in smart cities?

answers.a: Improved traffic flow and reduced congestion
answers.c: Reduction in the number of roads

explanations.a: Correct. Optimized signals improve mobility, reduce delays, and increase transportation efficiency.
explanations.d: The objective is to reduce waiting time.
